fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Utah Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Utah - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity utah, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to utah, speech-language pathologist compact utah, utah speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Division of Professional Licensing — Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: Utah Department of Commerce, Division of Professional Licensing (DOPL)
board.mailing_address: 160 East 300 South, Salt Lake City, Utah 84111
board.physical_address: 160 E. 300 South, Salt Lake City, UT 84111

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by September 30 of even-numbered years)

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in Utah.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Meet Educational Requirements
steps.description: Obtain a master's or doctoral degree in Speech-Language Pathology from a program accredited by the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA).

steps.title: Complete Clinical Fellowship or Hold ASHA CCC
steps.description: Complete a supervised clinical fellowship year (CFY) of at least 1,260 hours over a minimum of 36 weeks, OR hold the Certificate of Clinical Competence (CCC) from ASHA.

steps.title: Pass National Examination
steps.description: Pass the national examination in speech-language pathology (Praxis).

steps.title: Create MyLicense Account and Apply
steps.description: Register an account on the MyLicense portal (secure.utah.gov/account/login) and complete the online application for a Speech-Language Pathologist license.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Upload required documents such as official transcripts, Praxis scores, and CFY/ASHA CCC verification directly through the MyLicense portal or arrange for their submission by third parties.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $120.00 via the MyLicense portal to finalize your submission.

telepractice_text: Utah permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Utah issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Utah is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a Utah SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: $100 initial. $52 biennial renewal — one of the lowest renewal fees in the country.

faqs.question: Is Utah part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Utah allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Utah have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Utah participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Utah without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Utah?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Utah while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Utah is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Utah
hero.dek: To apply for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in Utah, submit your application and supporting documentation through the Utah DOPL's MyLicense online portal.
